frontmatter.title: Responsibility Driven Design Revisited – Ian Cooper | Craft 2026
frontmatter.description: Ian Cooper reconstructs responsibility-driven design as DDD's missing modeling technique, then demonstrates roles, CRC cards, collaborations, and delegated control with a QuestWorlds contest runner.

nodes.excerpt: AI Native Engineering – Ian Thomas (Talk Outline) A Craft 2026 talk by Ian Thomas, a UK-based (Yorkshire) software engineer at Meta…

frontmatter.title: AI Native Engineering – Ian Thomas | Craft 2026
frontmatter.description: Ian Thomas on how his Meta Horizon team drove AI-tool adoption from ad hoc to 95%+ — a leadership '5x productivity' challenge, Meta's monorepo scale and 'code wins arguments' culture, anchoring on Engineering Excellence, a six-dimension Sit-to-Leap maturity model run as a self-assessment workshop, the anti-test-slop CLI, concrete high-ROI patterns (data-mined test coverage, legacy refactoring, MCP domain knowledge, agentic code mods), platforms as glue (DRS, Confucius), and a deep Q&A.

nodes.excerpt: Coding Agents Don’t Scale Themselves – Patrick Debois (Talk Outline) A Craft 2026 (second-day afternoon) talk by Patrick Debois — known for…

frontmatter.title: Coding Agents Don't Scale Themselves – Patrick Debois | Craft 2026
frontmatter.description: Patrick Debois (the 'father of DevOps') on 'what if context was more like code?' — a full context development life cycle (generate, test/lint/eval/security-scan, package/version/registry, distribute, observe, feedback) — plus enabling agents across four layers (developer, team, platform, organization), the 'build the thing that builds the thing' mindset, and context drift/rot/authority as unsolved problems.

nodes.excerpt: AI & I: The Architecture of Our Identity – Xin Yao (Workshop Outline) A Craft 2026 interactive workshop (~20–30 people, 90 minutes) by Xin…

frontmatter.title: AI & I: The Architecture of Our Identity – Xin Yao | Craft 2026
frontmatter.description: Xin Yao's 90-minute interactive Craft 2026 workshop on identity when AI commoditizes knowledge — a fist-finger 'collaborative calculator' check-in, Joe Hudson's VIEW model of self-connection, a head/heart/gut identity architecture, and an event-storming-style possibles/lovables/undiscussables conversation café harvested into themes and tensions.

nodes.excerpt: Taste: The main advantage in AI – Tejas Kumar (Talk Outline) A Craft 2026 talk by Tejas Kumar (pronounced “like contagious”) — a developer…

frontmatter.title: Taste: The main advantage in AI – Tejas Kumar | Craft 2026
frontmatter.description: Tejas Kumar on taste as trainable, science-corrected pattern-matching — its origin in infant pattern-matching, how it goes wrong (bias/prejudice), a working definition (tacitly judging quality against external standards), worked examples (counterfeit money, centering, relative pitch), why taste drives business value, three protocols to train it (Kahneman & Klein's immersion + cues + corrective feedback, externalize a standard, cross-pollinate), Pixar's Brain Trust, web taste standards (INP/LCP/CLS, WAI-ARIA/WCAG, time-to-value), and live demos of streaming, MCP, and MCP apps.

nodes.excerpt: Computer vision beyond cameras – how robots can see with radars? – Andras Palffy (Talk Outline) A Craft 2026 talk by Andras (“Andy”) Palffy…

frontmatter.title: Computer vision beyond cameras - how robots can see with radars? – Andras Palffy | Craft 2026
frontmatter.description: Andras Palffy (co-founder, Perceive AI) makes the case for radar as the underrated robot-perception sensor — cheap, weather-robust, 3D, hideable — with the physics of why it sees through smoke/fog, live lidar-vs-radar demos, why radar data is so hard, the three things Perceive builds with radar only (ego-motion, occupancy, classification), applications from delivery robots to dual-use drone interception, and a wide-ranging Q&A.

nodes.excerpt: Agents Need Names: Trust at the age of agentic web – Balázs Némethi (Talk Outline) A Craft 2026 talk by Balázs Némethi (Hungarian…

frontmatter.title: Agents Need Names: Trust at the age of agentic web – Balázs Némethi | Craft 2026
frontmatter.description: Balázs Némethi (agent.community) argues AI agents will need names, identity, discovery, and trust as they become economic actors — the RPG-hero naming analogy, why platform handles are siloed, trust as the 'first hop', capability cards with provenance and selective disclosure, protocols (MCP/A2A), the open DNS-based Agent Identity & Discovery (AID) spec, agent-onboarding flows, and the campaign to make the .agent TLD a community-governed public good via ICANN — plus a three-question Q&A.

frontmatter.title: Tier 0 Engineering at Tesco Technology – Csorvási & Raksimowicz | Craft 2026
frontmatter.description: Tesco engineers explain how 13 people operate a five-billion-call-per-day tier-0 traffic platform using real-Envoy validation, cell deployments, synthetic SLIs, practiced recovery, and cautious AI adoption.

nodes.excerpt: Renegotiating Work in the AI Era – Zach Pendleton (Talk Outline) A Craft 2026 talk by Zach Pendleton (Instructure, an education-technology…

frontmatter.title: Renegotiating Work in the AI Era – Zach Pendleton | Craft 2026
frontmatter.description: Zach Pendleton (Instructure) argues AI is a culture accelerant, not a magic pill, and lays out how to deploy it effectively (start with goals, ~7.6% real productivity), sustainably (spend reports, plan-then-execute model tiering, protect human attention), and human-centered (AI councils, hack weeks, Avocode) — with the Turkey tutor-bot study, the 50-PR cautionary tale, repo context/skill libraries, and a Q&A on measuring productivity and junior growth.

nodes.excerpt: Diversity in the AI age – Panel discussion (Panel Outline) A Craft 2026 panel discussion (moderated by Gabriella) on tech diversity — gender…

frontmatter.title: Diversity in the AI age – Panel discussion | Craft 2026
frontmatter.description: A Craft 2026 panel on whether AI changes diversity in tech or just recreates old patterns — panelists from Tesco, MSCI, IBM, and the conference team on role models over access, bias starting in education/parenting, concrete workplace practices (part-time by default, diverse interview panels, women-only meetups, bring-your-kids), the positive-discrimination/impostor-syndrome dilemma, AI training-data bias, and whether 'female' management exists.

nodes.excerpt: The Awareness Layer – Robert Ranson (Talk Outline) A Craft 2026 talk by Robert Ranson — a sculptor/artist who found himself “of service to…

frontmatter.title: The Awareness Layer – Robert Ranson | Craft 2026
frontmatter.description: Robert Ranson argues the next big wave in agentic engineering is outward-facing — an always-on 'awareness layer' that senses the market so signal-to-action latency becomes the competitive moat. Covers the Agentics Foundation community, the Opus 4.1→4.5/4.6 shift, why building is 'nearly free' during a temporary deflation, turning muscles into senses, the living SWOT, sense-decide-act with an experimental fabric, engineers as Brazil's HVAC hero, and a wide-ranging Q&A on displacement, middle management, judgment, and trust.

frontmatter.title: Forest & Desert & Genie – Kent Beck | Craft 2026
frontmatter.description: Kent Beck examines AI-assisted development through forest and desert mindsets, balancing features with future options while navigating grief, teamwork, uncertain practice, and a blank industry playbook.

nodes.excerpt: AI Won’t Eat Your Stack – Randy Shoup (Talk Outline) A Craft 2026 talk by Randy Shoup — his ~11th Craft (“my very favorite conference in the…

frontmatter.title: AI Won't Eat Your Stack – Randy Shoup | Craft 2026
frontmatter.description: Randy Shoup on why free code generation won't let us rewrite everything — the psychological (Dunning-Kruger, inappropriate generalization), economic ('free like a puppy'), and completeness fallacies behind the '$300B SaaSpocalypse', with implications (glue code is free, domain expertise wins, modular components, option value) and predictions that components, services, and smaller players win.

nodes.excerpt: LLM projects: same or different?! - Sandor Kazi (Talk Outline) A Craft 2025 technical talk by Sandor Kazi — data solution architect at EPAM…

frontmatter.title: LLM projects: same or different?! - Sandor Kazi | Craft 2025
frontmatter.description: Sandor Kazi (EPAM data solution architect) warns software teams what surprises them when they start building with LLMs — the chainsaw/cake misalignment, the 'adequate solution too quickly' trap, consent & user education, reproducibility as the master key, control via programmatic switches and LLM-as-judge, Python-or-not, why input→output testing fails (determinize vs. fuzzy testing, deleted model versions, TDD breaking down), the 'data contradicts language' laptop stock_type example, providing context (prompt/fine-tune/agents), and cost control — structured around functional and non-functional requirements, plus a Q&A on package managers and judging.

nodes.excerpt: From here to there and back again - Simon Wardley (Keynote Outline) A Craft 2025 keynote by Simon Wardley — former CEO, researcher, twice…

frontmatter.title: From here to there and back again - Simon Wardley | Craft 2025
frontmatter.description: Simon Wardley's keynote on Wardley mapping — the origin story (Sun Tzu, Boyd's OODA), maps vs. graphs, evolution and the three pattern families, anticipating change (Blockbuster inertia, Jevons paradox, cloud→serverless→AI industrialization), how AI is changing coding, the argument that 'the code is the architecture' and that coding is craft while testing is engineering, and a closing on tools/medium/language, theocracies, and sovereignty.

frontmatter.description: A Craft 2025 diversity & inclusion panel moderated by Gabriella Machkolár, with Noémi (Tesco), Arina (EPAM) and Zach Pendleton (Instructure), on why diverse teams win, AI bias in hiring and the EU AI Act, widening the pipeline, loneliness at senior levels, the KPMG 'hard work' finding, merit vs. KPIs, cross-cultural teams, neurodiversity, and an audience pledge.

frontmatter.title: ESSENTIALS - There's No Such Thing As Plain Text - Dylan Beattie | Craft 2025
frontmatter.description: Dylan Beattie traces text from mechanical ASCII and incompatible code pages through Unicode, collation, normalization, UTF-8, and emoji to show that every text representation embeds technical, cultural, and political choices.

nodes.excerpt: Billion-Scale Triumph: Innovating at the Edge of Cloud Data - Balázs Szántó (Talk Outline) A Craft 2025 talk by Balázs Szántó, senior data…

frontmatter.title: Billion-Scale Triumph: Innovating at the Edge of Cloud Data - Balázs Szántó | Craft 2025
frontmatter.description: Balázs Szántó (Accenture) on a 3+ petabyte on-prem (Cloudera Impala/Kudu) to cloud migration on Databricks + Azure SQL Hyperscale handling ~4 billion records/day — architecture (bronze/silver/gold/platinum, Hyperscale internals), technology/concurrency validation, the PySpark repartition-by-range gotcha, clustered columnstore index and tuple-mover outages (up to 12 hours), the metadata-flush disaster, and hard-won lessons.

nodes.excerpt: The engineering strategist journey - Aleix Morgadas (Talk Outline) A Craft 2025 talk by Aleix Morgadas — engineering-strategy consultant and…

frontmatter.title: The engineering strategist journey - Aleix Morgadas | Craft 2025
frontmatter.description: Aleix Morgadas shares his personal journey into engineering strategy — the two failed modes (execute fast, copy others), following the money to understand the business, the maps analogy, what strategy actually is (decisions under imperfect data), strategy as a fractal cycle that's both deliberate and emergent, Rumelt's components, the Cynefin frame, a concrete full-stack-team turnaround story, and how success (not effort) reshapes culture.

frontmatter.title: AI as a Catalyst, Not a Replacement - Erik Slooten | Craft 2025
